event_blocks.summary: Inside a brightly lit auto repair shop, a mechanic wearing black gloves works on a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, identified as a Honda HR-V receiving its first oil change and an EPS recall at 6,000 miles. The mechanic first inspects the engine bay, unscrews the blue cap of the coolant reservoir, and confidently pours bright blue coolant from a jug, stating "no spills, never have spilled, and I never will spill. Just nothing but perfection." After securing the cap and commenting "That's looking good," the mechanic proceeds to the front driver-side wheel, initiating the loud, rapid "rat-a-tat-tat" of a yellow impact wrench to remove lug nuts from the silver alloy wheel with a Hankook tire. The mechanic detaches the tire, referred to as "them wheelie boys," revealing brake components, and a solid thud is heard as it's set down. The mechanic then rolls the tire across the concrete floor, past the lift's safety arms, and places it on the ground, remarking "Magneto Tray from STS Auto Design. Link in my bio." After returning to the vehicle and briefly showing the exposed suspension and brake assembly, the mechanic confirms, "Already checked the tire pressures, the tire tread. All that is looking good," concluding with an exclamatory "Yeah!" The cluttered environment includes toolboxes, a workbench, and a red air compressor, all under fluorescent lighting.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves, performing wheel removal and narrating his actions in a male voice. He identifies himself or his product as 'Magneto Tray from STS Auto Design' and mentions a 'Link in my bio,' also making a casual 'Fuck you' remark and confirming he has checked tire pressures and tread.

toolboxes and workbench.description: Black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench with various supplies, and a red air compressor are visible in the background.

Hankook tire.description: A black tire with 'Hankook' branding, removed from the hub with a thud and then rolled across the floor, producing rolling sounds.

yellow impact wrench.description: Used to remove lug nuts, producing distinct rapid, percussive mechanical sounds.

coolant reservoir.description: Located in the engine bay, identifiable by its blue cap; filled with bright blue coolant from a large jug, described by the mechanic as 'wishy-wash fluid' with 'no spills.'

silver alloy wheel.description: Five-spoke design, shiny and clean, attached to the front driver-side of the sedan; removed to expose brake components.

dark gray sedan.description: Elevated on a blue hydraulic lift with its hood open; front driver-side wheel is removed during the segment, exposing the brake components.

coolant jug.description: Large translucent plastic jug containing bright blue coolant, labeled with product information; used to refill the reservoir.

blue hydraulic lift.description: Supports the sedan, with yellow safety arms visible; part of the shop’s equipment.

raw_segments: The video opens from a first-person perspective inside a brightly lit auto repair shop, where a dark gray 2024 Honda HR-V, identified by the mechanic as a "Honda Herby" getting its "first oil change" and an "EPS recall" at "6,000 miles," is elevated on a blue hydraulic lift with its hood open. The mechanic, wearing black gloves, begins by inspecting the engine bay. As they unscrew the blue cap of the coolant reservoir, the mechanic describes the process, pouring bright blue "wishy-wash fluid" from a large translucent jug, confidently stating "no spills, never have spilled, and I never will spill. Just nothing but perfection." After securing the cap, the mechanic confirms, "That's looking good," and continues with commentary like "analyzing weight and trajectory" and "Cappuccino, remove a kiss." The environment is cluttered with tools, toolboxes, and automotive equipment, including a green workbench and a red air compressor, all illuminated by overhead fluorescent lights. The mechanic then moves to the front driver-side wheel, initiating the loud, rapid "rat-a-tat-tat" sounds of a yellow impact wrench as they remove the lug nuts. The camera focuses closely on the silver alloy wheel with a Hankook tire, which the mechanic refers to as "them wheelie boys" while "rotisserating" them, before detaching it to reveal the brake components underneath. The mechanic's deliberate movements and final commentary ("Release") indicate experience and focus throughout the task.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a mechanic, wearing black gloves, in a well-lit auto repair shop. The distinct, rapid, percussive sounds of a yellow impact wrench immediately fill the audio as the mechanic approaches a dark gray sedan, elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, and systematically removes the lug nuts from its silver alloy wheel. After a final burst of the wrench, the mechanic pulls the Hankook tire off the hub, revealing the brake components, and a solid thud is heard as the tire is removed. The mechanic then rolls the tire across the concrete floor, past the yellow safety arms of the lift and other shop equipment, with the rolling sound audible. As the mechanic carries the tire to a nearby area, a male voice, presumably the mechanic, casually exclaims "Fuck you," before identifying himself or his product, stating, "Magneto Tray from STS Auto Design. Link in my bio." He continues to narrate his actions, saying, "Set you right here. Come here. Set you right up on over here. A little criss-cross in the front. Set you right back here," as he places the tire on the ground and returns to the vehicle. The camera briefly captures the exposed suspension and brake assembly, including the caliper and rotor. The mechanic then adds, "Pick it. Set you over here," and confirms, "Already checked the tire pressures, the tire tread. All that is looking good," before a final exclamatory "Yeah!" is heard, indicating satisfaction with the progress. The shop environment, with visible toolboxes, a red air compressor, and other vehicles, remains consistent throughout.

event_blocks.summary: A mechanic, wearing black gloves, works on a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, initially focusing on the front driver-side wheel well where a silver alloy wheel has been removed. The mechanic uses a yellow impact wrench, producing whirring and impacting sounds, to remove lug nuts, stating, "We'll do that for later," and "You and you." Transitioning to a first-person view, the mechanic moves under the vehicle, employing a red and green cordless impact wrench to loosen undercarriage bolts, repeatedly saying "Release" as fasteners are removed. After detaching an undercarriage panel, which lands with a clank, the mechanic instructs, "Set that down gently." The mechanic then retrieves a blue-handled ratchet, identified as "Matt cool ratchet" with a "17 milson" socket, and proceeds to the rear of the sedan. Near a metallic, mesh-wrapped exhaust system component, the mechanic uses the long ratchet, its rhythmic clicking audible, to address the original drain plug torque, remarking, "See how hard it is to break loose the original drain plug torque here," then "Not hard at all," and finally, "That's a proper torque there." The bright auto repair shop environment, featuring blue walls, other vehicles on lifts, and toolboxes and workbench, provides the backdrop for these precise actions.

red and green cordless impact wrench.description: Used by the mechanic to remove bolts from the undercarriage; its distinct whirring and impacting sounds are heard during operation.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves, performing wheel removal and undercarriage work; their voice is heard giving instructions and commentary such as 'We'll do that for later,' 'You and you,' 'Release,' 'Set that down gently,' 'Matt cool ratchet,' '17 milson,' 'Flippy flip,' 'Double flip, trip flip. Oops,' 'See how hard it is to break loose the original drain plug torque here,' 'Not hard at all,' and 'That's a proper torque there,' indicating experience and focus.

undercarriage panel.description: A gray plastic panel removed from the underside of the sedan; the mechanic instructs to 'Set that down gently' as it's placed on the floor, accompanied by a clanking sound.

Hankook tire.description: Black tire with 'Hankook' branding visible on the sidewall, mounted on a silver alloy wheel; removed during the segment and rolled across the floor.

blue-handled ratchet.description: A ratchet with a blue handle and silver head, used by the mechanic to loosen bolts on the undercarriage; the mechanic refers to it as 'Matt cool ratchet' and mentions using a '17 milson' (likely a 17mm socket) with it, accompanied by rhythmic clicking sounds.

yellow impact wrench.description: Used to remove lug nuts from the front driver-side wheel; its distinct whirring and impacting sounds are heard during operation.

silver alloy wheel.description: Five-spoke design, shiny and clean, attached to the front driver-side of the sedan; removed to expose brake components; later seen rolled across the floor.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a dark gray sedan's front driver-side wheel well, elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, with the silver alloy wheel removed, exposing brake and suspension components. The mechanic, wearing black gloves, uses a yellow impact wrench to remove lug nuts, accompanied by the distinct whirring and impacting sounds of the tool. The mechanic's voice is heard saying, "We'll do that for later," and then, "You and you," as they work. The camera then shifts to a first-person perspective as the mechanic moves underneath the vehicle, using a red and green cordless impact wrench to remove bolts from the undercarriage, with more impact wrench sounds. The mechanic repeatedly says "Release" as fasteners are loosened. After removing an undercarriage panel, which is then placed on the floor with a clanking sound, the mechanic instructs, "Set that down gently." Returning to the toolboxes, the mechanic selects a blue-handled ratchet, which they refer to as "Matt cool ratchet" and mention "17 milson" (likely a socket size). The scene shifts to the rear of the vehicle, where the mechanic uses the long ratchet near a metallic, mesh-wrapped exhaust system component, accompanied by the rhythmic clicking of the ratchet. The mechanic comments on the task, saying, "See how hard it is to break loose the original drain plug torque here," and then, "Not hard at all," concluding with, "That's a proper torque there." Throughout, the bright auto repair shop environment, with blue walls, other vehicles on lifts, and toolboxes, is visible, highlighting the mechanic's precise and focused movements.

event_blocks.summary: From a first-person perspective, a mechanic wearing black gloves works under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, with dark, used oil draining into a blue oil pan. The mechanic, narrating his actions, uses a red and green cordless impact wrench, its whirring and impacting sounds audible, to remove a bolt from the oil pan, then removes the blue oil filter with a specialized wrench, humorously calling a component a "Dingly magneto on a stick" and noting the "Filtration system." He installs a new filter, describing the process as "Buttery smooth," securing it by hand before tightening it with a blue-handled ratchet, its clicking sounds accompanying his strained grunts of effort. The camera pans the undercarriage, revealing a mesh-wrapped exhaust system component and suspension parts, with toolboxes, a green workbench, and other equipment visible in the well-lit garage. The mechanic offers a "quick little ratchet flip tutorial" and tightens a bolt near the exhaust system to "30 foot pounds." He sprays a component with brake cleaner from a red can labeled 'Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE,' describing the residue, then removes the blue oil pan filled with used oil and installs a new blue oil filter with the ratchet. He wipes down the area, commenting "Raggy poo," "Nice little wipe down here," and "Nice looking, real fucking nice," before concluding the task.

red and green cordless impact wrench.description: Used by the mechanic to remove bolts from the oil pan; features a red body with green accents and a built-in light; held in the mechanic’s gloved hand.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves, performing an oil change under the vehicle; speaks in a conversational, instructional, and sometimes expletive-laden tone, providing commentary on the process and demonstrating techniques; uses a blue-handled ratchet and oil filter wrench; first-person perspective suggests camera is mounted on the mechanic’s head or chest.

toolboxes and workbench.description: Black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench with various supplies, and a red air compressor are visible in the background; the mechanic selects tools from the toolbox during the segment.

blue-handled ratchet.description: A ratchet with a blue handle and silver head, used by the mechanic to tighten the new oil filter and other components; its distinct clicking sounds are heard as the mechanic demonstrates a 'ratchet flip tutorial' and applies torque.

exhaust system component.description: Metallic, mesh-wrapped exhaust component visible under the vehicle; located near the rear suspension; the mechanic works nearby while changing the oil filter.

oil filter.description: Blue cylindrical filter, removed and replaced by the mechanic; the new filter is installed and tightened with a ratchet, with the mechanic commenting on the 'beautiful threads' and applying '30 foot pounds' of torque.

blue hydraulic lift.description: Supports the sedan, with yellow safety arms visible; remains in place throughout the segment; part of the shop’s equipment.

undercarriage panel.description: A gray plastic panel removed from the underside of the sedan; placed on the floor after removal; features multiple mounting holes and cutouts.

Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can.description: Red can labeled 'Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE' used by the mechanic to clean or degrease components under the vehicle; the sound of spraying is clearly heard as the mechanic applies it, referring to the residue as 'Rasputin hair'.

raw_segments: The video opens with an upbeat, jazzy tune playing as a mechanic, seen from a first-person perspective and wearing black gloves, works under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. Dark, used oil is actively draining into a large blue oil pan positioned beneath the vehicle. The mechanic narrates his actions in a calm, clear voice, stating "Really, uh, release" and preparing to "gracefully pivot this." He then uses a red and green cordless impact wrench, its distinct whirring and impacting sounds audible, to remove a bolt from the oil pan, followed by the removal of a blue oil filter using a specialized wrench. He humorously refers to a component as a "Dingly magneto on a stick" and notes the "Filtration system" as he replaces the old filter with a new one. Describing the installation as "Buttery smooth," he secures the new filter by hand before tightening it with a blue-handled ratchet, whose clear clicking sounds are heard as he applies force, making strained grunts of effort. The camera pans around the undercarriage, showing a metallic, mesh-wrapped exhaust system component and surrounding suspension parts, while black toolboxes, a green workbench, and other equipment are visible in the well-lit, organized garage background. The mechanic's precise and deliberate movements indicate experience and focus. He concludes the tightening, confirming "That'll do," and prepares to reattach the oil pan, with the blue oil pan still positioned below to catch any residual oil.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a mechanic, wearing black gloves, working under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. The mechanic's conversational and instructional voice is heard as he begins by tapping a component, saying, "give her a little love tap, okay." He then mentions a "New washer for ya" while using a blue-handled ratchet on the oil pan area, accompanied by its distinct clicking sounds. He offers a "quick little ratchet flip tutorial," demonstrating the technique with verbal instructions like, "you're gonna want to just go like this... and then you go like this... and then if you do that, you go like that," followed by, "that's how you do that." The camera pans around the well-lit garage, revealing toolboxes, a green workbench, and a red air compressor. The mechanic then refers to a "Nice little trickle for ya," likely indicating the dark, used oil draining into the blue oil pan positioned below. He slides a component into place, saying, "Slide that bad boy in there," and notes, "Beautiful threads on there" while tightening a bolt near the mesh-wrapped exhaust system, grunting with effort and stating, "30 foot pounds." He then picks up a red can labeled 'Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE' and sprays it on a component, describing the residue as "Bit of the Rasputin hair here... and a little hair," with the clear sound of spraying. After cleaning, he removes the blue oil pan filled with used oil and handles a new blue cylindrical oil filter, installing it with the ratchet. The gray plastic undercarriage panel, previously removed, is seen on the floor. The mechanic continues his methodical work, wiping down the area and commenting, "Raggy poo," "Nice little wipe down here," and finally, "Nice looking, real fucking nice," before concluding with "Fuck out of here" and "Something," as he finishes the task, showcasing his skill in the organized chaos of the auto repair shop.

event_blocks.summary: From a first-person perspective, a mechanic wearing black gloves works under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, performing an oil change and filter replacement in a well-lit garage. The mechanic uses a red and green cordless impact wrench to remove bolts from the oil pan, playfully referring to them as "twisty boys" and emphasizing the importance of replacing removed hardware. After selecting a blue-handled ratchet from a black toolbox on a green workbench, the mechanic returns under the vehicle to tighten a new blue cylindrical oil filter, applying lubricant from a white tube, humorously calling it "Pepto Bismol" and "cappuccino" to the filter's gasket. The mechanic also uses a Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can, accompanied by a hissing sound, to clean components and refers to various parts with colorful analogies like "rubber dingus" and "Magneto pocket Scrooge." The blue oil pan collects draining oil, and a 'USED OIL' container is visible nearby. The mechanic concludes by tightening the new filter with the ratchet, remarking on its secure fit and calling the final tightening a "love tap," underscoring the methodical nature of the task.

red and green cordless impact wrench.description: Used by the mechanic to remove bolts from the oil pan; features a red body with green accents and a built-in light; held in the mechanic’s gloved hand. The mechanic refers to it as 'this guy' and 'another Envision Auto 3D tool'.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves, performing oil change under the vehicle; uses a blue-handled ratchet, red and green cordless impact wrench, and lubricant tube; first-person perspective suggests camera is mounted on the mechanic’s head or chest. The mechanic speaks throughout the segment, narrating his actions and playfully describing parts and processes with phrases like 'Going in?', 'rubber dingus', 'springy boy', 'Pepto', 'Cappuccino', 'jingle jangle', and checking for 'toit and roit' tightness, also mentioning 'Envision Auto 3D tool' and 'Honda bond'.

toolboxes and workbench.description: Black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench with various supplies, and a red air compressor are visible in the background; the mechanic selects tools from the toolbox during the segment.

undercarriage panel.description: A gray plastic panel removed from the underside of the sedan; placed on the floor after removal; features multiple mounting holes and cutouts.

blue-handled ratchet.description: A ratchet with a blue handle and silver head, used by the mechanic to tighten the new oil filter; selected from the toolbox and used in multiple locations. The mechanic uses it to ensure the filter 'feels toit and roit'.

Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can.description: Red can labeled 'Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE' used to clean or degrease components under the vehicle; held by the mechanic and sprayed on parts.

The mechanic playfully refers to the new filter as a 'springy boy' and applies lubricant to it, describing the process with terms like 'Bit of Pepto' and 'Cappuccino'.

blue hydraulic lift.description: Supports the sedan, with yellow safety arms visible; remains in place throughout the segment; part of the shop’s equipment.

used oil container.description: A blue wheeled container labeled 'USED OIL' in white text, positioned near the workbench and used for collecting drained oil.

green workbench.description: A mobile workbench with a bright green tray, holding tools, a yellow funnel, a metal tray, and a plastic bag containing a new oil filter; positioned near the mechanic during the oil change process.

exhaust system component.description: Metallic, mesh-wrapped exhaust component visible under the vehicle; located near the rear suspension; the mechanic works nearby while changing the oil filter.

raw_segments: The video begins with a first-person perspective of a mechanic, wearing black gloves, working under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, performing an oil change. As he uses a red and green cordless impact wrench to remove bolts from the oil pan, positioned underneath to catch the draining oil, the mechanic provides a running commentary. He points out "nice little tabs" and "twisty boys" (fasteners), playfully stating, "We got one for you" multiple times with each bolt removed. He then addresses other technicians, emphasizing the importance of putting "back the hardware that you took out of the vehicle," calling it a "little tech tip." After selecting a blue-handled ratchet from an organized black toolbox at the workbench, the mechanic returns under the vehicle. He uses the ratchet to tighten a new blue cylindrical oil filter, and a Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can to spray and clean components, accompanied by its distinct hissing sound. The undercarriage panel, a gray plastic piece, is visible, having been removed earlier. The well-lit shop environment, with other vehicles and equipment like a silver alloy wheel and Hankook tire, forms the background. The mechanic's movements are deliberate, with close-ups on his gloved hands manipulating tools and parts. The segment concludes with the mechanic using the impact wrench again, referring to the final tightening as a "love tap," and remarking, "See? That's all it takes. It's not that hard, is it?" underscoring the methodical and straightforward nature of the task.

From a first-person perspective, the video opens under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, revealing the undercarriage as a mechanic, wearing black gloves, works on the oil pan. Amidst the constant hum and occasional clinks of the garage, the mechanic observes an "EPS recall" and notes being "down a glove fingertip" before playfully declaring "I am speed" while using a red and green cordless impact wrench to efficiently remove bolts. The scene shifts to a green workbench displaying various tools, including a yellow funnel and a Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can. The mechanic then prepares a new oil filter, returning under the vehicle to apply lubricant from a white tube, humorously referring to it as "Pepto Bismol" and "a smidge of this" for an "injectus device." After installing the filter, the mechanic uses a blue-handled ratchet to tighten it securely, also mentioning a "rubber dingus" and applying more grease. Throughout the methodical process, punctuated by the mechanic's casual commentary and tool sounds, the blue oil pan collects draining oil, and a 'USED OIL' container is visible, highlighting the organized and efficient oil change.

From a first-person perspective, the mechanic, wearing black gloves, works under a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift in a well-lit garage. The mechanic begins by using a red and green cordless impact wrench, stating, "Shack that bad boy loose," to remove a component near the oil pan. After a few more turns, the mechanic scrapes off some "old Honda bond," confirming, "Good enough." They then retrieve a new blue cylindrical oil filter from the green workbench, which holds various tools, a yellow funnel, and a metal tray. The mechanic humorously refers to a tool as an "Invision Auto 3D finger wheel" and a part as "cappuccino" while working, expressing satisfaction with "Yep, this is good. Things are happening." As they continue, they exclaim, "Gotcha, bitch," while removing another part, which they refer to as a "Magneto pocket Scrooge" and a "cap spring cappuccino," adding it to a "collection." The mechanic then applies lubricant, humorously calling it "3 ccs of Pepto Bismol," from a white tube with a red cap to the new oil filter’s gasket. They describe other components as a "forky deal" and a "rubber deely" before sliding the new filter into place, confirming, "That should be good." Finally, a blue-handled ratchet is used to securely tighten the new oil filter. Throughout the precise, step-by-step oil filter replacement, the mechanic moves between the undercarriage and the workbench, with the blue oil pan catching draining oil and a 'USED OIL' container visible nearby.

The segment opens with soft, melancholic piano music playing as the mechanic, seen from a first-person perspective and wearing black gloves, works under the dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. He uses a blue-handled ratchet to tighten a component, asking "Going in?" and confirming "Okay." He then pulls out a part, remarking, "Pull that rubber dingus back out. Yeah. Done with you. Done with you." Moving to the green workbench, he selects a new blue cylindrical oil filter from a plastic bag. Applying lubricant from a white tube with a red cap to the filter's gasket, he playfully describes the process: "And now we need this springy boy. Bit of Pepto on the springy boy. Cappuccino. Something like that." Returning under the vehicle, he installs the new oil filter, guiding it into place with a "jingle jangle" and instructing to "stick your finger in there." He then tightens it with the ratchet, checking its security and stating, "Make sure she feels toit and roit. And that's feeling pretty tight. And that's also feeling pretty right. I like that." The mechanic also briefly uses a red and green cordless impact wrench and sprays components with a red Brake Cleaner FLAMMABLE can. He concludes by grabbing another tool, mentioning "another Envision Auto 3D tool right here," and preparing to apply "a little bit of a Honda bond." Throughout the process, the blue oil pan catches draining oil, and a 'USED OIL' container is visible, all within a well-lit garage with various tools and equipment.

event_blocks.summary: A mechanic, wearing black gloves, begins by selecting a red-handled tool from a green workbench tray, as a voiceover discusses working with 'threads.' The camera then focuses on the undercarriage of a dark gray sedan, elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, where the mechanic uses the red-handled tool near the oil pan, accompanied by metallic scraping and threading sounds, with the voiceover stating, 'we're just gonna slide this right in.' The mechanic identifies the tool as a 'beautiful 3D printed tool made by Envision Auto 3D,' designed for 'Honda EPS recalls,' and suggests a link for 'Honda tech' lacking it. Returning to the workbench, the mechanic retrieves a blue-handled ratchet and uses it to tighten a component under the car, producing distinct clicking sounds, as the voiceover instructs to 'tighten her up smidge here.' The mechanic then accesses a 'ratchet vault' and identifies a 'beautiful digital quarter inch torky wrench,' concluding a maintenance procedure in a well-lit garage. Subsequently, the mechanic uses a blue-handled ratchet to tighten a component under the sedan, stating, 'cranking her down to 9 foot-pounds,' clarifying, 'which is going to be a 6 foot-pounds using this tool,' and expresses satisfaction. Moving to a black toolbox, the mechanic selects a long silver socket wrench and proceeds to the front driver-side wheel, using a yellow impact wrench to remove lug nuts from the silver alloy wheel. The Hankook tire is detached and rolled away, revealing brake components. The mechanic then uses a red-handled tool to adjust the brake assembly, followed by the whirring and ratcheting sounds of the yellow impact wrench for re-installing lug nuts. The video then shows the mechanic removing lug nuts from the silver alloy wheel with the yellow impact wrench, noting 'Buttery smooth lug nuts,' before detaching the Hankook tire. The mechanic selects a blue-handled ratchet from a toolbox and returns to the vehicle, observing a potential issue with a tool's performance. Later, the mechanic retrieves a coolant jug from the green workbench and refills the coolant reservoir using a yellow funnel, noting '4.4 quarts of oil.' The mechanic then moves to the car's interior, turning on the ignition to illuminate the dashboard and Honda logo. The engine starts, and the mechanic uses a yellow-handled tool to remove the oil cap and dipstick in the engine bay, noting 'the oil life already reset.' After closing the hood, the mechanic uses a high-pressure water hose to wash the car's front. Returning to the engine bay, the mechanic uses a blue-handled ratchet to remove a component near the oil pan, followed by a red and green cordless impact wrench and a red-handled tool on the oil pan and surrounding components. The video concludes with the mechanic continuing engine work amidst ongoing mechanical and impact wrench sounds.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves and dark pants, operating from a first-person perspective; uses a yellow-handled tool, blue-handled ratchet, and red-handled tool to remove the oil cap, dipstick, and work on the oil pan. A male voice, presumably the mechanic's, provides commentary throughout the process, saying things like "Starts right up," "oil life already reset," "let that juice flow," "little raggy poo," "show me what you got," and "This went well."

coolant reservoir.description: Located in the engine bay, identifiable by its blue cap; filled with bright blue coolant from a large jug.

interior of the sedan.description: Dashboard illuminated with various warning lights and gauges; Honda logo visible on the steering wheel; beige and black interior with a digital display and center console. The sound of an engine starting is heard from within.

coolant jug.description: A large translucent plastic jug containing bright blue coolant, its use is accompanied by the distinct sound of liquid pouring as the mechanic refills the reservoir.

yellow-handled tool.description: Used to remove the oil cap from the engine; held by the mechanic’s gloved hand.

red hose reel.description: Mounted on the wall, holding the high-pressure water hose; features a red frame and a coiled black hose; used to supply water for washing the car.

oil fill port.description: A circular opening on the engine cover labeled 'SAE 0W-20,' where oil is added; visible after the oil cap is removed.

engine bay.description: Exposed with various components visible, including the oil fill port labeled 'SAE 0W-20,' oil dipstick, and surrounding hoses and connectors.

red and green cordless impact wrench.description: Used by the mechanic to remove bolts from the oil pan; features a red body with green accents and a built-in light; held in the mechanic’s gloved hand, it produces a distinct, rapid impact sound during its operation.

oil dipstick.description: A metal rod with a handle, removed and inspected by the mechanic to check oil level; reinserted after inspection.

high-pressure water hose.description: Used to wash the front of the car; sprays water across the hood, grille, and front bumper; connected to a red hose reel mounted on the wall, its operation is accompanied by the distinct sound of spraying water.

raw_segments: From a first-person perspective, a mechanic wearing black gloves begins by selecting a red-handled tool from a green workbench tray, while a male voice describes working with 'threads' and needing a 'bare finger right upon them threads.' The camera then moves to the undercarriage of a dark gray sedan, elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, where the mechanic uses the red-handled tool near the oil pan, accompanied by metallic scraping and threading sounds, as the voice states, 'we're just gonna slide this right in' with 'zero issues.' The mechanic identifies this as a 'beautiful 3D printed tool made by Envision Auto 3D,' specifically for 'Honda EPS recalls,' and encourages viewers to click a link if they are a 'Honda tech' without this tool. After a brief return to the workbench, the mechanic retrieves a blue-handled ratchet and returns to the vehicle, using it to tighten a component under the car, with distinct ratchet clicking sounds, as the voice instructs to 'tighten her up smidge here.' The mechanic then mentions going into the 'ratchet vault' and identifies a 'beautiful digital quarter inch torky wrench,' concluding the focused maintenance procedure in the well-lit garage environment.

The video begins with a close-up of the mechanic, wearing black gloves, using a blue-handled ratchet to tighten a component under the dark gray sedan, which is elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. The mechanic's voice is heard, stating they are "cranking her down to 9 foot-pounds," accompanied by distinct clicking sounds, before clarifying, "which is going to be a 6 foot-pounds using this tool." The undercarriage is exposed, revealing parts of the exhaust system and suspension. The mechanic expresses satisfaction, saying, "We like that. We're happy with that. And we're going to roll with that." Moving to a black toolbox, the mechanic selects a long silver socket wrench, with clanking sounds of tools, and walks toward a green workbench where various tools and supplies, including a yellow funnel and a plastic bag containing a new oil filter, are laid out. The mechanic places the wrench on the workbench, remarking, "Need our half-inch torque boy, 19 mil," amidst more tool clanking. The mechanic then proceeds to the front driver-side wheel of the sedan, using a yellow impact wrench to remove the lug nuts from the silver alloy wheel, which features a five-spoke design. The mechanic declares, "Done with these guys. And we're done with you," and then, "And we're going to set you right here. Fuck you," seemingly addressing a component or tool. The Hankook tire is then detached and rolled across the concrete floor, accompanied by the mechanic saying "Wheels" and then grunting and making scraping sounds as they state, "Back on. Back on you go, bud." The mechanic inspects the brake components behind the wheel, including the brake caliper and rotor, before placing the wheel aside. The camera briefly pans to show the shop environment, including a red air compressor, a blue used oil container, and other vehicles on lifts in the background. The mechanic then returns to the wheel, using a red-handled tool to adjust or inspect the brake assembly, and is heard saying "You... some lug nuts" as the distinct whirring and ratcheting sounds of the yellow impact wrench begin, followed by clicks, indicating the re-installation or tightening of lug nuts. The scene ends with the mechanic continuing to work on the brake components, with the blue hydraulic lift and yellow safety arms visible in the background.

The video opens with a close-up of the dark gray sedan's front driver-side wheel, elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. The mechanic, wearing black gloves, begins removing lug nuts from the shiny five-spoke silver alloy wheel with a yellow impact wrench, its distinct ratcheting and air tool sounds filling the garage. The mechanic's calm, clear male voice is heard remarking, "Some more lug nuts," and then, "Into your home," as the process continues. After a series of impact wrench bursts, he humorously notes, "Buttery smooth lug nuts," before adding, "Come here," as he detaches the Hankook tire and rolls it across the concrete floor, revealing the brake components. The camera then pans to the well-lit garage environment, showcasing black toolboxes and a green workbench with various supplies, including a yellow funnel. The mechanic walks towards the toolboxes, selects a blue-handled ratchet from an organized drawer, and returns to the vehicle. As the impact wrench sounds resume, the mechanic observes, "Hmm. My stub is not wanting to feather very good," indicating a slight issue with the tool's performance while preparing to work on the brake system in the wheel well. The segment concludes with the mechanic's final, "Okie dokie," as the work progresses.

The minute opens with an upbeat, slightly jazzy background music as a first-person perspective shows a mechanic, wearing black gloves and dark pants, in a well-lit auto repair shop. A clear male voiceover begins, stating, "I'm with you, and we're down with you, and we're ready for you," continuing to describe a "Sure Shot folder from STS" as a potential "digital torque wrench holder." Visually, the mechanic reaches for a blue-handled ratchet from a nearby toolbox, then moves to the front driver-side wheel of a dark gray sedan, which is elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. As the voiceover mentions "if you're brave enough," a distinct, high-pitched whirring sound emerges, immediately followed by rapid, metallic impacts as the mechanic attaches a yellow impact wrench to the silver alloy wheel's lug nuts. The voiceover continues, speculating about holding "regular torque wrenches too," and later stating "80 foot pounds" and "yep." Throughout this, the mechanic efficiently removes the lug nuts one by one, each deliberate action punctuated by the loud, rhythmic impacts and whirring of the impact wrench. After all nuts are removed, the mechanic detaches the Hankook tire from the wheel hub and rolls it across the concrete floor, briefly exposing the vehicle's undercarriage and brake components. The mechanic then uses the blue-handled ratchet to loosen additional fasteners near the wheel well, while the distinct whirring and rapid metallic impacts of the impact wrench continue in the background, occasionally punctuated by the voiceover's "Mhm." The environment remains consistent with visible toolboxes, a green workbench, and a red air compressor, as the mechanic’s precise and efficient movements continue amidst the ongoing mechanical sounds and promotional speech.

The video opens with a first-person perspective of a mechanic, wearing black gloves and dark pants, working on a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. The distinct whirring and impacting sounds of a yellow impact wrench are heard as the mechanic efficiently removes lug nuts from the front driver-side wheel. The mechanic occasionally vocalizes, confirming actions with an 'Mhm' and later stating, 'Thank you for your service,' possibly to the tool or the car. After removing the wheel, the mechanic walks to a well-organized toolbench, selecting a blue-handled ratchet from a black toolbox filled with neatly arranged tools. The scene then shifts to the engine bay, where the mechanic retrieves a large translucent coolant jug from a green workbench. With the sound of liquid pouring, the mechanic refills the coolant reservoir with bright blue coolant using a yellow funnel, noting, 'Uh, 4.4 quarts of oil. And we got that shit on tap. Okay.' He then playfully instructs, 'Get your ass up in here,' and later warns, 'Dangerous games here, bud. Dangerous games,' while continuing his work. As he places the jug back, he gives himself instructions like 'Pivot,' 'Reposition,' and 'Push transfer,' before humorously exclaiming, 'Cappuccinius, whoa, cappuccino.' The mechanic then declares, 'Done with you,' to various components, before moving to the car's interior. The dashboard lights up as the ignition is turned on, revealing the Honda logo on the steering wheel and an illuminated instrument cluster, with the mechanic inviting, 'Come on up in here.' Throughout the segment, a calm, instrumental background music plays, complementing the deliberate and focused movements of the mechanic in the well-lit auto repair shop, which features blue and white walls, various tools, a red air compressor, and a yellow safety arm on the lift.

The video begins with a first-person view from inside a dark gray sedan, showcasing the illuminated dashboard with the Honda logo on the steering wheel and various gauges. The interior features beige and black upholstery, and a digital display on the center console reads 'No Device Connected.' An engine starts up, and a male voice comments, "Starts right up." The camera then shifts to the engine bay, where a mechanic’s black-gloved hand uses a yellow-handled tool to remove the oil cap. The mechanic proceeds to remove the oil dipstick, inspect it, and then reinsert it, while the male voice notes, "And we have the oil life already reset... There she is. All right. Just let that uh juice flow for a minute here." After the hood is closed, the male voice says, "Okay. Come back over here. Little raggy poo." The mechanic then uses a high-pressure water hose, producing a distinct spraying sound, to wash the front of the car, spraying water across the hood, grille, and front bumper. The male voice continues, "And if you could just uh show me what you got. Oil, like that. Okay." The camera pans around the vehicle, showing the dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift in a well-lit garage. The male voice remarks, "This went well." After washing, the mechanic returns to the engine bay, where the oil cap is now open, revealing the oil fill port labeled 'SAE 0W-20.' Mechanical clanking and ratcheting sounds begin as the mechanic uses a blue-handled ratchet to remove a component near the oil pan. A red and green cordless impact wrench is then used on the oil pan, producing a distinct, rapid impact sound. The mechanic also uses a red-handled tool, likely a wrench, to work on a component near the oil pan. The video ends with the mechanic continuing to work on the engine, with the oil pan and surrounding components visible, amidst ongoing mechanical and impact wrench sounds.

event_blocks.summary: A mechanic, wearing black gloves, works within the engine bay of a dark gray sedan elevated on a blue hydraulic lift, interacting with components like the oil fill port and oil dipstick amidst a low, sustained synth sound. Metallic clanking and rattling sounds accompany the mechanic's actions, punctuated by a male voice saying "Linda." The synth sound fades as the camera reveals the sedan's front and pans across a well-lit auto repair shop featuring other vehicles on lifts, black toolboxes, a green workbench, and a red air compressor. A calm instrumental music track begins, accompanying the scene until it transitions to a black screen displaying the 'TE VIDEOS' logo and 'WE LIKE THAT' text, accompanied by smiling, nodding images of a man with curly hair and a mustache, while the instrumental music continues.

oil dipstick.description: A metal rod with a handle, removed and inspected by the mechanic to check oil level; reinserted after inspection.

mechanic.description: Wearing black gloves and dark pants, operating from a first-person perspective; their actions with tools, including a yellow-handled tool, blue-handled ratchet, and red-handled tool, are accompanied by metallic clanking and rattling sounds.

yellow-handled tool.description: Used to remove the oil cap from the engine; its use is implied by the metallic clanking and rattling sounds heard during the mechanic's work.

toolboxes and workbench.description: Black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench with various supplies, and a red air compressor are visible in the background; the mechanic selects tools from the toolbox during the segment.

high-pressure water hose.description: Used to wash the front of the car; sprays water across the hood, grille, and front bumper; connected to a red hose reel mounted on the wall.

man with curly hair and mustache.description: Appears twice on either side of the 'WE LIKE THAT' text; has voluminous curly brown hair, a prominent mustache, and wears a light blue collared shirt; his head is slightly tilted and he smiles warmly.

interior of the sedan.description: Dashboard illuminated with various warning lights and gauges; Honda logo visible on the steering wheel; beige and black interior with a digital display and center console.

WE LIKE THAT text.description: Bold white text appearing below the TE VIDEOS logo, flanked by two identical images of a man with curly hair and a mustache, who is smiling and nodding.

dark gray sedan.description: Elevated on a blue hydraulic lift; front driver-side wheel being serviced; undercarriage partially visible after tire removal.

red hose reel.description: Mounted on the wall, holding the high-pressure water hose; features a red frame and a coiled black hose; used to supply water for washing the car.

TE VIDEOS logo.description: White circular logo with 'TE VIDEOS' text inside a hexagonal shape, displayed on a black background.

oil fill port.description: A circular opening on the engine cover labeled 'SAE 0W-20,' where oil is added; visible after the oil cap is removed.

blue hydraulic lift.description: Supports the sedan, with yellow safety arms visible; remains in place throughout the segment.

engine bay.description: Exposed with various components visible, including the oil fill port labeled 'SAE 0W-20' and the oil dipstick, where metallic clanking and rattling sounds are heard during the mechanic's work.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a first-person perspective of a mechanic, wearing black gloves, diligently working on the engine bay of a dark gray sedan, which is elevated on a blue hydraulic lift. A low, sustained synth sound begins to play. As the mechanic interacts with engine components like the oil fill port and dipstick, a male voice distinctly says "Linda," followed by metallic clanking and rattling sounds, suggesting the use of tools for an oil change or inspection. The synth sound then fades out. The camera pulls back to reveal the full front of the sleek Honda sedan, then pans across the well-lit auto repair shop, showcasing other vehicles on lifts, black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench, and a red air compressor, including a silver SUV. A new, calm instrumental background music track begins. The scene concludes with a transition to a black screen displaying the white 'TE VIDEOS' logo and the bold 'WE LIKE THAT' text, flanked by two identical images of a smiling, nodding man with curly hair and a mustache, while the instrumental music continues.

global_entity_library.mechanic: Wearing black gloves and dark pants, operating from a first-person perspective; their actions with tools, including a yellow-handled tool, blue-handled ratchet, and red-handled tool, are accompanied by metallic clanking and rattling sounds.
global_entity_library.toolboxes and workbench: Black toolboxes with organized tools, a green workbench with various supplies, and a red air compressor are visible in the background; the mechanic selects tools from the toolbox during the segment.
global_entity_library.Hankook tire: Black tire with 'Hankook' branding visible on the sidewall, mounted on a silver alloy wheel; removed during the segment and rolled across the floor.
global_entity_library.yellow impact wrench: Used to remove lug nuts from the front driver-side wheel, its distinct whirring and impacting sounds are clearly audible during operation.
global_entity_library.coolant reservoir: Located in the engine bay, identifiable by its blue cap; filled with bright blue coolant from a large jug.

The mechanic playfully refers to the new filter as a 'springy boy' and applies lubricant to it, describing the process with terms like 'Bit of Pepto' and 'Cappuccino'.

global_entity_library.lubricant tube: A white tube with a red cap, labeled 'Lubricant', used by the mechanic to apply grease to the new oil filter before installation. The mechanic humorously refers to the lubricant as 'Pepto' and 'Cappuccino'.
global_entity_library.red-handled tool: A red-handled tool, likely a wrench or socket, used by the mechanic to work on a component near the oil pan; retrieved from the green workbench tray, its use is part of the general mechanical sounds.
